frame_label: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
generated_comment: Personally I prefer women who keep it natural. A little is fine, but when someone is doing full makeup daily and then says it's only for herself, it feels a bit fake yaar. At the same time, basic grooming matters too — if you don't take care of yourself at all, that also says something.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it applies a double bind to women's appearance: condemning visible makeup as fake while also expecting 'basic grooming.' It encodes the natural-vs-artificial policing and dismisses women's stated reasons for makeup by implying it is deceptive or performative.
original_frame: Prenup, Alimony as Reverse Dowry, Women
persona_text: You are Rohit. Age 28. Single. A software engineer in Pune. You are in a Reddit thread on r/India where someone posted about a man who has to pay 80000 rupees per month in alimony to his ex wife who has a job. You are commenting anonymously and sharing your opinion on whether you will ever get married given laws like this and what you think women's actual motivations for marriage are.
